meta.title: Smart Energy — HMI Evaluation (XR / Quest3 condition)
meta.description: Operator performs N-1 security assessment using the XR HMI on Meta Quest3. XR flow differs from 2D: element selection and contingency toggling are spatially integrated, timestamp and run are on the same panel, and results appear simultaneously. Paired with pilot_se_hmi_eval_2d_v0.
meta.revision_note: v0.2 — workflow rebuilt from XR app screenshots. Original 10-step 2D flow does not apply to XR. XR has 6 steps.

task_parameters.note_rt_max: Must match 2D condition (120s) for cross-condition metric comparison.
task_parameters.note_baseline: Must match 2D condition (95s) to isolate interface effect in EL.

objects.name: GridGlobe3D
objects.note: 3D globe with green pins for substations and coloured line markers. Operator taps a pin to open the BUS_PANEL.

objects.name: BusDetailPanel
objects.note: Floating panel showing bus metadata and connected lines. Each line has a red toggle button — tapping it deactivates the line (contingency) and adds it to the SIM_PANEL list.

objects.name: SimulationControlPanel
objects.note: Persistent floating panel with date/time spinners (year/month/day/hour/minute), list of deactivated lines, and blue Run Simulation button. Trash icon clears all contingencies.

objects.name: BusResultsPanel
objects.note: Centre floating table. Appears simultaneously with RESULTS_LINE after simulation run.

objects.name: LineResultsPanel
objects.note: Right floating table. Appears simultaneously with RESULTS_BUS. Shows loading_percent per line.

workflow.description: 6-step XR workflow. Derived from screenshots of the running app. Key structural differences from 2D: (1) element selected before simulation, not after; (2) contingency defined by toggling lines in bus detail panel; (3) timestamp + run are on the same panel; (4) bus and line results appear simultaneously, not as separate views.
xr_vs_2d_key_differences: XR step 2 (select bus) happens BEFORE the simulation — in 2D, element selection (step 9) happens AFTER
XR has no separate base case run — operator goes directly to contingency setup
XR timestamp selection and Run Simulation are on the same panel, after contingency is defined
XR bus + line results appear simultaneously — 2D has separate tabs inspected sequentially
XR Operator Agent not yet implemented (step 10 from 2D workflow is absent in current XR version)

steps.label: Open environment
steps.xr_action: App launches, 3D globe with grid markers appears
steps.note: Session boundary. Captured by start_time in session header. No decision event.

steps.label: Select bus on globe
steps.xr_action: Operator physically taps a green substation pin on the 3D globe

payload_required.element_id: bus number from panel header e.g. '16'

steps.note: interaction_id starts here. Carries through to step 6.

steps.label: Toggle line(s) off — define contingency
steps.xr_action: Operator taps red toggle button next to a line in the BUS_PANEL

payload_required.line_id: line number from panel row e.g. '12'

steps.note: One event per line toggled. If operator toggles two lines, emit two contingency_defined events. interaction_id carries over from step 2.

steps.label: Set timestamp and run simulation
steps.xr_action: Operator adjusts date/time spinners on SIM_PANEL, then physically touches the blue Run Simulation button

payload_required.lines_deactivated: array of line IDs currently toggled off e.g. ['12', '15']

steps.note: duration_s = time from last contingency_defined to Run Simulation press. interaction_id carries over.

steps.label: Inspect results
steps.xr_action: Bus results (centre) and line loading results (right) appear as simultaneous floating panels. Operator reads both.
steps.note: ONE event for both panels — they appear simultaneously in XR. duration_s = time from panel render to next operator action. interaction_id carries over.

steps.label: Operator decision
steps.xr_action: Operator concludes inspection. Accepts result, or clears contingency (trash icon) and repeats with different line.

payload_required.lines_deactivated: array of line IDs in this cycle

steps.correct_derivation: correct = whether operator assessment matches SecurityAI classification from UBI. Cleared contingency + repeat cycle = implicit override = new interaction_id.
steps.note: interaction_id closes here. Trash + new bus tap = new cycle with new interaction_id.

expected_differences.F: XR emits fewer events per cycle by design — F will be structurally lower. Normalise by cycle count not raw event count for fair comparison.
expected_differences.D: XR step 5 may show higher dwell — operator processes bus and line results simultaneously
expected_differences.HCL: Open research question — XR spatial integration may lower or raise cognitive load
expected_differences.EL: Primary hypothesis: XR reduces total decision time vs 2D → lower EL
expected_differences.S: Sequence order differs structurally — S will capture this divergence as a feature, not noise
